title: Port backlog.* and capture.* into the dispatcher; bee_backlog.mjs + bee_capture.mjs become shims
action: Per DB1-DB3 and the du-1 mechanism: add registry entries + handlers for backlog add/counts/rank/badges and capture add/list/flush/count against lib/backlog.mjs and lib/capture.mjs; preserve every pinned message (e.g. backlog add's exact validation refusals like severity P1|P2|P3, four-verb Use: line) and exit code. Replace both helper bodies with the group shim. Copy to .bee/bin siblings. Extend bijection coverage to both groups.

truths: all existing test_lib backlog/capture CLI checks pass unchanged through the shims
bee.mjs backlog add / capture add accept the same flags with the same refusal texts
prohibitions: no logic changes in lib/backlog.mjs or lib/capture.mjs

trace.outcome: Ported backlog.* (counts/rank/badges/add) and capture.* (add/list/flush/count) into bee.mjs's registry+handlers; bee_backlog.mjs and bee_capture.mjs are now thin group shims over main(). Extended FLAG_ALONE_BOOLEANS with write, added GROUP_USAGE_FALLBACKS for both groups, and extended GROUP_HELPERS + both group whitelists + bijection/example coverage in test_bee_cli.mjs.
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 220 passed, 0 failed | test_bee_cli.mjs: 90 passed, 0 failed | test_onboard_bee.mjs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 1
